hard.next_step: feb 20, 2026: A two-hour, in-person session is scheduled for February 23 at Johanesberg , to include IT and procurement stakeholders. Demo Preparation Face-to-face demo booked for Monday, February 23 (2-hour session) Zycus to share deck and Showpad link; Nedbank to review, specify focus modules and participants, and coordinate building access Jan 23, 2026: Caroline presented Zycus S2P - keen on demo to follow up: focused on spend analytics & forecasting, supplier risk/rating & media monitoring, and RFP/tender process automation
hard.ais_why:
• Jan 2026 Avoma notes capture Zycus presenting a 'modular Source-to-Pay platform spanning eProcurement, eInvoicing, spend analytics, sourcing, contract management, and supplier risk with embedded Generative AI', plus Spend Analytics with 'Merlin Insight generative chatbot' and a buyer-side action item to 'send a comprehensive deck, banking use cases, and a knowledge document about how Agentic AI can be useful for financial institutions'. The banking-Agentic-AI knowledge document ask is a buyer-originated AI signal.
• Formal Evaluation stage, BD Engaged. A large face-to-face session was scheduled Feb 23, 2026 at Sandton Sun with ~15 Nedbank stakeholders (Vanessa Carolus, Kandis Swanepoel, Nombuso, Hemanth, IT and procurement reps) plus banking-peer references Capitec, Bank of Cyprus and ING. Last activity Feb 23, 2026. Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope alongside the full S2P suite plus ML iAnalyze.
• High confidence: Avoma evidence is explicit on Agentic AI and Merlin Insight; banking-focused AI follow-ups have been committed by Zycus. Held at 8 (not 9-10) because the customer requested banking case studies first ‚Äî AI interest is real but not yet a formal selection criterion or POC. A confirmed AI-banking POC or RFP-with-AI-mandate would push to 9-10.

north_star_verdict.headline: Genuinely interested BFSI buyer ran a 15-stakeholder onsite demo on 23 Feb, then went quiet: Parleen's 21 May follow-up drew only an auto-reply, no economic buyer is mapped, and the deal is stalling on procurement inertia rather than a named rival.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-01-20 (168 days old). Moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation 2026-03-11, so ~117 days in current stage. Close date 2027-01-20 is ~198 days out, so the runway is healthy and the date is not the problem. Last logged activity 2026-05-21 was a Zycus-side follow-up email that received only an out-of-office auto-reply; the last genuine two-way buyer engagement was the 23 Feb onsite demo, ~133 days ago. No forward slip in field history. The math does not force Off Track, but a live thread gone quiet for four-plus months after a strong demo makes this Slowing.
evidence: LastActivityDate 2026-05-21 was Parleen Kour's outbound 'Continuing the Conversation After Our Onsite Demo' email; the only reply was Hemant Harrielall's auto-reply (SF activities)
Last genuine buyer engagement was the 23 Feb 2026 Sandton Sun onsite demo to ~15 stakeholders (AIS_Why + Next_Step)
Stage Formal Evaluation since 2026-03-11 (field history), ~117 days in stage with no RFP, shortlist, or commercials signal
No Economic Buyer or C-level contact role; senior procurement contacts are Kandis Swanepoel (Head of Procurement Delivery) and Vanessa Carolus (Head of Procurement COE), both quiet since the demo
Close date 2027-01-20 is ~198 days out, so timing is not the issue; engagement is
AIS 8 Strong: buyer requested a banking Agentic-AI knowledge document (20 Jan call) but this has not converted to a POC or AI-mandated RFP

metrics.narrative: Nedbank articulated clear qualitative pain on the 20 Jan discovery call but never quantified it. Vanessa Carolus (Head of Procurement COE) described manual, fragmented work across spend analytics, supplier risk and RFP workflows, saying 'everything at the moment is manual... it's taking a lot of stitching of information in order to get something out', and a finance-side speaker probed working-capital and payment-term scenario analysis. No savings target, headcount, cycle-time or error-rate baseline was stated on the call or logged in Next_Step__c, tasks or AIS notes. That missing value case, ~168 days into the deal, is the single biggest barrier to a future economic-buyer approval, so quantifying expected S2P savings with procurement finance is a ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in any Salesforce contact role, field, or call. The most senior buyer voices are procurement leaders, not budget owners: Kandis Swanepoel (Head of Procurement Delivery) controlled the meeting agenda and logistics on 20 Jan ('I would prefer another call because I think there's key people that's not on this call... I'll ask the managers to extend the invite'), and Vanessa Carolus (Head of Procurement COE) owns the risk/CoE agenda. Neither is the likely sign-off authority for a $300k SaaS commitment at a bank; a CPO or CFO sits above them and has never appeared. Identifying and reaching that budget owner, most plausibly via Kandis, is a top move, because commercial approval cannot happen until they are engaged.
sources: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Kandis Swanepoel, Vanessa Carolus)
SF OpportunityContactRole (no EB/Exec Sponsor role present)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated concrete evaluation criteria on 20 Jan, confirmed by the 23 Jan Next_Step summary: spend analytics and forecasting (including predictive/scenario analysis on categories like diesel volume and unit price), automated supplier risk rating and adverse-media monitoring, and RFP/tender process automation. Momokete Nkgau (tender hub manager) framed tenders as the acid test: 'once we receive the deck... I will compare how we do our tenders and see what the system can do'. SAP S/4HANA integration is an explicit technical criterion Vanessa raised. These are confirmed asks, but they have not yet been formalised into a scored RFP or BRD, so weighting and pass/fail thresholds remain inferred.
sources: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Vanessa Carolus, Momokete Nkgau)

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself reveals the process: a 20 Jan intro call with procurement (Kandis, Vanessa, Momokete) escalated into a ~15-person, two-hour onsite demo on 23 Feb that Kandis deliberately widened to include IT and procurement stakeholders ('there's also no one from our IT... key people that's not on this call that need to be part of this discussion'). That shows Nedbank runs a committee evaluation and can convene one when motivated. What is missing is the formal path AFTER the demo: no RFP, shortlist, or approval chain is documented, and there has been no logged decision-process step since 23 Feb, which is why the deal now reads as stalled rather than advancing.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible: no MSA, SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec track has been discussed, which is expected for an early-stage Formal Evaluation deal with no commercials yet. Salesforce does, however, name the people who will run paper later: Liezel Mcleod (Head of Legal, Risk and Compliance) and Shamanth Reddy (Contract Manager) are on the opportunity as contact roles, and as a South African bank Nedbank will require DORA-style vendor compliance and a full security review before signature. Visibility will come only once the buyer re-engages and an evaluation outcome forces a commercial and legal path.
sources: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Caroline referenced DORA compliance for financial institutions)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and owned in procurement. On 20 Jan Vanessa Carolus described three operational pains: manual, stitched-together spend analytics and forecasting; manual supplier risk tiering done 'on an Excel model' with no automated media monitoring; and slow, manual RFP/tender workflows. Momokete Nkgau confirmed tenders are still run manually even inside their current system. Vanessa also flagged integration pain from past tool rollouts: 'we find when we are bringing on tools that the integration tends to be challenging because we've customized so much.' The pain is real and articulated, but it remains qualitative, and its owners (procurement) are not the budget owner.
sources: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Vanessa Carolus, Momokete Nkgau)

champion.narrative: Vanessa Carolus (Head of Procurement COE) is the working champion: on 20 Jan she drove the agenda, articulated the pain, and pushed to add the supplier-risk and media-monitoring topics to the next session. Kandis Swanepoel (Head of Procurement Delivery) was arguably the stronger internal mobiliser, insisting on widening the demo to IT and coordinating access and invites. Both showed genuine early advocacy by convening ~15 colleagues for the 23 Feb onsite. But the rating is only 'developing' and now at risk: since the demo there has been no internal-selling signal, no economic-buyer introduction, and no reply to the 21 May follow-up beyond an auto-reply, so we cannot confirm the champion is still carrying the deal. Re-confirming Vanessa's and Kandis's stance is the first move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Vanessa Carolus, Kandis Swanepoel)
SF activity 21 May 2026 (Parleen Kour follow-up; only auto-reply returned)

competition.narrative: There is no rival vendor named in any source: no Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, or Jaggaer appears in the call, fields, tasks, or Next_Step. The real competition is the status quo. Nedbank confirmed it runs 'Ariba and obviously the SAP for our spend', does supplier risk tiering manually in Excel, and runs tenders manually, so the fight is between a Zycus purchase and either extending SAP Ariba or continuing to do nothing. The wedge is Zycus's AI: Merlin Insight for spend analytics, iRisk with continuous supplier/media scanning, and automated RFP generation, backed by the prebuilt S/4HANA adapter to defuse the integration objection. The risk is inertia, not a competitor: after a strong demo the buyer has simply gone quiet, which favours do-nothing.
sources: SF Products__c (Merlin, ML iAnalyze, iRisk in scope)

critical_signals.text: The 23 Feb Sandton Sun onsite demo pulled in roughly 15 Nedbank stakeholders including IT, but produced no logged buyer feedback, and the only reply to Parleen's 21 May follow-up was an out-of-office auto-reply.
No rival vendor has surfaced; the deal is a fight against do-nothing inertia and an SAP Ariba status quo that Nedbank confirmed it still runs alongside manual spend and tender work.
Procurement leaders Kandis Swanepoel (Head of Procurement Delivery) and Vanessa Carolus (Head of Procurement COE) are engaged, but no CPO or CFO budget owner has ever been met.
No pricing, ROI, or savings figure has been discussed on a $300k deal, so there is no quantified value case to justify the spend to a future economic buyer.

deal_movement.summary: Strong Jan-Feb build (intro call, then a 15-stakeholder onsite demo and an amount bump to $300k) followed by a four-plus-month buyer silence; the only touch since 23 Feb is a Zycus-side follow-up that drew an auto-reply.

items.change: Onsite two-hour demo delivered to ~15 Nedbank stakeholders (procurement + IT) at Sandton Sun
Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation
Parleen Kour sent a post-demo follow-up email; only an out-of-office auto-reply returned, no buyer engagement since

competitive_position.summary: No rival vendor is named in any source; the single strongest current threat is do-nothing inertia. Nedbank confirmed on 20 Jan it still runs SAP Ariba plus manual Excel-based supplier risk and manual tenders, so the deal is Zycus versus extending the SAP status quo. The four-month post-demo silence favours no-decision, not a competitor. The wedge is Zycus's embedded AI (Merlin Insight, iRisk, automated RFP) delivered without the integration pain via the prebuilt S/4HANA adapter.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (manual processes)
competitors.quote: everything at the moment is manual... it's taking a lot of stitching of information in order to get something out
competitors.how_we_win: Convert AI appetite (AIS 8) into a scoped spend-analytics + supplier-risk proof so inaction becomes visibly costly; re-engage with the banking Agentic-AI knowledge doc.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba (incumbent, inferred)
competitors.quote: We're using Ariba and obviously the SAP for our spend
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI and automated RFP against Ariba's rules-based workflows, and use the prebuilt S/4HANA adapter to neutralise the customisation/integration objection Vanessa raised.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer's three stated focus areas map cleanly to Zycus modules; the one exposed area is SAP S/4HANA integration, which was claimed (prebuilt adapter) but never validated with Nedbank's SAP Enterprise Architect.

items.source: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Vanessa Carolus / finance speaker)

no savings, cycle-time, or headcount figure stated by the buyer

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded into procurement after a strong-but-stale demo; the win levers are re-activating the champions, mapping the budget owner, and closing the SAP integration objection.
flags: Multi-thread beyond procurement to the CPO/CFO budget owner: 168 days in, no economic buyer has ever been engaged, so any future commercial has no sponsor.
Re-activate the silence with the buyer-requested banking Agentic-AI proof (Capitec reference + AI knowledge document) since AIS 8 shows the AI story is the strongest wedge.
Neutralise the SAP S/4HANA integration objection by getting Michiel Visser (SAP Enterprise Architect) into a technical validation session before any commercial step.

items.name: L Mcleod
items.title: Head of Legal, Risk and Compliance

champion_strength.summary: Vanessa Carolus (Head of Procurement COE) drove the pain narrative and demo request on 20 Jan, and Kandis Swanepoel (Head of Procurement Delivery) mobilised a ~15-person onsite on 23 Feb, so early advocacy was real. But it is only developing and now at risk: no internal-selling signal, no economic-buyer introduction, and no reply beyond an auto-reply to the 21 May follow-up. What is missing is a re-confirmed, active champion carrying the deal to power.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 20 Jan 2026 (Vanessa Carolus, Kandis Swanepoel); SF activity 21 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard and effectively with AI on 20 Jan (Merlin Insight spend chatbot, iRisk continuous supplier/media scanning, automated RFP generation and contract redlining), and the buyer pulled on it by requesting a banking Agentic-AI knowledge document. AIS 8 Strong is well supported; the AI narrative is the deal's strongest lever and is not under-positioned.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8 (Strong) is corroborated by the calls: the buyer asked for a banking Agentic-AI knowledge document and engaged on Merlin Insight and predictive spend analytics. Interest is genuine but has not yet become a formal AI selection criterion or POC, so it reads AI Hungry, not yet AI-mandated.

items.detail: No genuine buyer engagement since the 23 Feb onsite demo; the 21 May follow-up drew only an auto-reply, so the deal has stalled four-plus months in Formal Evaluation.
No economic buyer (CPO/CFO) mapped or engaged; single-threaded into procurement (Kandis Swanepoel, Vanessa Carolus).
Primary threat is do-nothing inertia: Nedbank still runs SAP Ariba plus manual spend and tender work and could simply not decide; no quantified value case exists to force action.
SAP S/4HANA integration concern raised by Vanessa Carolus remains unvalidated; SAP Enterprise Architect Michiel Visser has not been engaged.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled from a strong Jan-Feb run to four-plus months of buyer silence; the last touch was Zycus-side and unanswered. The deal is not dead (real pain, real AI appetite, a convened committee) but it is clearly cooling and needs re-activation.
items: No buyer reply since the 23 Feb onsite demo
21 May Zycus follow-up returned only an auto-reply
No RFP, shortlist, or commercials signal in 117 days in Formal Evaluation

items.trigger: 23 Feb onsite demo drew no logged feedback and the 21 May follow-up got only an auto-reply
items.expected_effect: Re-opens the silent thread and surfaces whether Nedbank is still evaluating, reviving momentum toward the next milestone.

items.trigger: No economic buyer mapped 168 days in; single-threaded to procurement
items.expected_effect: Maps the missing sign-off authority so a future commercial has a sponsor, lifting win probability.

items.trigger: Buyer requested banking case studies and an AI knowledge doc on 20 Jan; AIS 8 shows AI is the wedge
items.expected_effect: Uses the strongest lever (banking AI proof) to convert dormant interest into a re-engaged evaluation.

items.action: Offer Michiel Visser a technical validation session on the prebuilt S/4HANA adapter.
items.trigger: Vanessa flagged SAP integration and heavy customisation as a concern on 20 Jan
items.expected_effect: Neutralises the integration objection before it can block a commercial decision.

items.action: Propose a scoped spend-analytics and supplier-risk proof to quantify Nedbank's savings.
items.trigger: No quantified value case exists; do-nothing inertia is the primary threat
items.expected_effect: Builds the ROI evidence needed to make inaction costly and justify the $300k to the economic buyer.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely interested BFSI buyer ran a 15-stakeholder onsite demo on 23 Feb, then went quiet: no economic buyer is mapped, no rival is named, and the deal is stalling on procurement inertia rather than a competitor.

win_position.text: Nedbank showed real appetite: Kandis Swanepoel pulled Zycus into a ~15-person onsite demo at Sandton Sun on 23 Feb, asked for banking Agentic-AI proof, and the AI story is landing (AIS 8, Strong).
We are single-threaded into procurement (Kandis Swanepoel, Vanessa Carolus) with no CPO or CFO budget owner ever met, so there is no one to approve a $300k spend.
The buyer has gone silent since the February demo, replying only with an auto-reply to Parleen's 21 May follow-up, and there is no RFP, shortlist, or commercials in sight.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum reversed after a strong Jan-Feb run: no buyer touch since 21 May and no next meeting booked, leaving the deal stalled in Formal Evaluation.
The 23 Feb onsite did convene roughly 15 stakeholders including IT, proving Nedbank can assemble a real buying committee when it is motivated.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is unproven: no RFP issued, no shortlist confirmed, no pricing or timeline agreed, and no economic buyer named.
Nedbank invested two hours and ~15 people in the 23 Feb onsite and requested banking case studies, signalling genuine, if now-quiet, interest.

deal_risk.text: The primary risk is do-nothing inertia: Nedbank keeps running spend analytics, supplier risk and tenders manually on SAP/Ariba and could simply not decide.
The SAP S/4HANA integration concern Vanessa Carolus raised on 20 Jan is unaddressed, and SAP Enterprise Architect Michiel Visser has not been engaged.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer engagement since 23 Feb; 21 May Zycus follow-up returned only an auto-reply.

customer_commitment.evidence: Strong onsite investment on 23 Feb, but no RFP, pricing, or economic buyer since.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing inertia plus an unvalidated SAP integration objection and an unmapped budget owner.

scope_change.detail: Amount raised from $200k to $300k on 2026-02-22 (field history), with the full S2P suite plus Merlin and ML iAnalyze retained in scope; no module has been dropped.

customer_preference.evidence: Deep, Zycus-specific questions and a self-driven 15-person onsite on 23 Feb, but no stated preference over any rival and no Zycus-specific commercial next step; interest has since gone quiet.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain articulated (manual spend analytics, Excel-based supplier risk, manual tenders) but no savings, cycle-time, or headcount figure quantified.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 30 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 15 is anchored on the stage win (30), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Nedbank Ltd — Formal Evaluation · $300K · closes 20 Jan
cro_panel.intro: A genuinely interested BFSI buyer ran a 15-stakeholder onsite demo on 23 Feb, then went quiet; no economic buyer is mapped, no rival is named, and the deal is stalling on procurement inertia rather than a competitor

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.text: Nedbank showed real appetite; Kandis Swanepoel pulled Zycus into a ~15-person onsite demo at Sandton Sun on 23 Feb, asked for banking Agentic-AI proof, and the AI story is landing (AIS 8, Strong)
We are single-threaded into procurement (Kandis Swanepoel, Vanessa Carolus) with no CPO or CFO budget owner ever met, so there is no one to approve a $300k spend
The buyer has gone silent since the February demo, replying only with an auto-reply to Parleen's 21 May follow-up, and there is no RFP, shortlist, or commercials in sight

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum reversed after a strong Jan-Feb run; no buyer touch since 21 May and no next meeting booked, leaving the deal stalled in Formal Evaluation
The 23 Feb onsite did convene roughly 15 stakeholders including IT, proving Nedbank can assemble a real buying committee when it is motivated

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

The SAP S/4HANA integration concern Vanessa Carolus raised on 20 Jan is unaddressed, and SAP Enterprise Architect Michiel Visser has not been engaged

blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

Nedbank invested two hours and ~15 people in the 23 Feb onsite and requested banking case studies, signalling genuine, if now-quiet, interest
